Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 287 [sale of rab-banutu prebend][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ma!-ḫi-ri ša₂ ⸢3⸣-TA u₄-⸢mu-MEŠ⸣ lu₂GAL-DU₃u₂-tu

(o 1) [...] he gave 2/3 kurru 5 sūtu for 2 minas 2 shekels of silver with the ginnu-mark to Isu, son of Mukin-apli, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya, (as) the price of 3 days of rab-banûtu-prebend on the Harriṣu-canal which (is) at the Gate of Šamaš of the sector of Uruk; upper length adjacent to the city wall, lower length adjacent to the field belonging to the field of Šamaš-zer-lišir, son of Šamaš-iddin, descendant of Rab-bani.

o 22

[ina muḫ]-ḫi id₂ḫar-ri-ṣu ša₂ ina ⸢KA₂.GAL⸣ dUTU NAM UNUGki

o 33

US₂.SA.DU ANu₂ DA BAD₃ US₂.SA.DU KIu₂ DA A.⸢ŠA₃⸣

o 44

lib₃-bu-u₂ A.ŠA₃ ša₂ mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—MU A lu₂GAL-DU₃

o 55

⸢2/3?⸣ GUR 5(BAN₂)u₂ a-na 2 MA.NA 2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ gi-ni ⸢a-na⸣ mi-su DUMU-šu₂

o 66

ša₂ mDU—A A! mŠU—dna-na-a id-din-nu EGIR x x x [KU₃.BABBAR]

(o 6) Afterwards, [he gave], in the hands of Isu, son of Mukin-apli, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya, 1/2 mina 4 shekels [of silver] with the ginnu-mark from the income of the prebend (for a) total of 2 1/2 minas 6 shekels of silver of the field, [silver] with the ginnu-mark from the income of the prebend of Šamaš-zer-lišir, son of Šamaš-iddin, [descendant of Rab-bani], price (for) the 3rd days of the prebend of rab-banûtu, the price of 1 pānu 3 sūtu ...

o 77

ša₂ gi-ni te-eš₁₅-qi₂-tu₄ PAP 2 1/2 MA.NA 6 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ A.ŠA₃ [KU₃.BABBAR]

o 88

ša₂ gi-ni te-eš₁₅-qi₂-tu₄ mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—⸢MU⸣ [A lu₂GAL-DU₃]

o 99

⸢ŠAM₂⸣ 3-TA u₄-mu-MEŠ GIŠ.ŠUB lu₂GAL-DU₃u₂-tu ina ⸢ŠU⸣-[MIN mi-su]

o 1010

⸢A-šu₂ ša₂⸣ m⸢DU⸣—A A mŠU—dna-na-a ma-ḫir ša₂ 1 PI 3(BAN₂) x [...]

o 1111

ṣu-pur mdUTU—NUMUN—GIŠ ki-ma na₄KIŠIB-šu₂ tu-ud-⸢da⸣-[(a)-tu₂]

(o 11) The nail of Šamaš-zer-lišir is marked in lieu of his seal.

Reverse
r 1r 1

lu₂mu-kin-nu mgu-za-nu A-šu₂ ša₂ mpir-ʾi-šu₂ mNUMUN-⸢ia⸣ [A-šu₂ ša₂ m...]

(r 1) Witnesses: Guzanu, son of Pirʾišu; Zeria, [son of PN]; Ša-pi-Anu, son of Šamaš-eṭir, descendant of the "rab-banî, [PN], son of Šamaš-šum-ukin, descendant of Kidin-Marduk; Nabu-zer-lišir, [son of PN], descendant of Rimut-Anu; Nanaya-iddin, son of Nidinti-[...]; Libluṭ, son of Šamaš-zer-ibni, descendant of Babutu; [PN], son of Kudurrišu, descendant of Nur-Šamaš; Anu-eriba, son of Šamaš-reʾušunu; Šamaš-iddin, son of Šamaš-eṭir, descendant of Kidin-Marduk.

r 22

mša₂-pi-i—d60 A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—SUR A ⸢lu₂GAL-DU₃⸣ [...]

r 33

A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—MU—DU A mki-din—dAMAR.UTU mNA₃—NUMUN—GIŠ [A-šu₂ ša₂ m...]

r 44

A mAM—d60 mdna-na-a—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mni-din-ti—[d...]

r 55

mlib-luṭ A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—NUMUN—DU₃ A mba-bu-tu [m...]

r 66

A-šu₂ ša₂ mNIG₂.DU-šu₂ A ša₂ mZALAG₂—d30 md60—SU A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—⸢SIPA⸣-[šu₂-nu]

r 77

mdUTU—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ mdUTU—SUR A mki-din—dAMAR.UTU

(2 lines blank)
r 88

lu₂UMBISAG mARAD—dUTU A-šu₂ ša₂ md60—ŠEŠ—MU A men-nap-šu₂ UNUGki

(r 8) Scribe: Arad-Šamaš, son of Anu-ah-iddin, descendant of Ennapšu. At Uruk, month Arahsamnu, 18th day, 15th year, Darius, king of Babylon, king of the lands.

r 99

itiAPIN U₄ 18-KAM₂ MU 15-KAM₂ mda-a-ri-ia-muš

r 1010
